records.ety: [OE. abregen, OF. abregier, F. abrger, fr. L. abbreviare; ad + brevis short. See Brief and cf. Abbreviate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Abridged (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Abridging.]

defs: To make shorter; to shorten in duration; to lessen; to diminish; to curtail; as, to abridge labor; to abridge power or rights.
quotes: She retired herself to Sebaste, and abridged her train from state to necessity.

defs: To shorten or contract by using fewer words, yet retaining the sense; to epitomize; to condense; as, to abridge a history or dictionary.
To deprive; to cut off; -- followed by of, and formerly by from; as, to abridge one of his rights.
